evals.prompt: Implement Self-Attention Guidance (SAG) from the paper 'Improving Sample Quality of Diffusion Models Using Self-Attention Guidance' into a ComfyUI custom node. There is no reference code — use the paper description: during sampling, compute an attention map from the intermediate self-attention layers, create a blurred version of it, and use the difference as a guidance signal by modifying the noise prediction. The node should work as a drop-in modifier between a model loader and KSampler — it should take MODEL as input and output a patched MODEL. Expose parameters for: guidance scale, blur sigma, and which attention layers to extract from.
evals.expected_output: A ComfyUI custom node package at custom_nodes/comfyui_sag/ containing __init__.py, nodes.py, and attention_patches.py that implements SAG via model_options transformer_options patching. The node correctly clones the ModelPatcher, injects attention patches via set_model_options_patch_replace, extracts self-attention maps during sampling, computes a blur-difference guidance signal, and applies it. Files should follow ComfyUI node registration conventions with NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS.
expectations: Uses model.clone() before adding patches
Registers attention patches via patches_replace or set_model_attn1_patch
Reads from /comfy/ldm/modules/attention.py or greps /comfy/ for attention patterns
Uses extra_options to access sigma and block info
Produces correct NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS and __init__.py
Exposes guidance_scale, blur_sigma, and layer selection parameters in INPUT_TYPES
Outputs MODEL type (not a raw tensor)
Does not hardcode device or dtype assumptions

evals.prompt: Write a ComfyUI custom node that modifies the noise schedule mid-sampling. The node should take a LATENT, a MODEL, and a custom sigma curve defined by a tensor input (FLOAT values representing a list of sigma values). It should override the default sigma schedule with the user-provided one and run the sampling with it. The output should be the denoised LATENT. Make this a self-contained sampling node — not a KSampler modifier, but a node that performs its own sampling loop using ComfyUI's sample_custom or equivalent.
evals.expected_output: A ComfyUI custom node that takes MODEL, LATENT, CONDITIONING (pos/neg), and a custom sigma tensor, performs a full sampling loop with the custom schedule, and returns a denoised LATENT. The node uses comfy.sample.sample_custom() or comfy.samplers with model.model_options. It correctly handles the LATENT dict contract (preserving noise_mask), uses KSampler or equivalent, and properly converts user-provided sigma values to the model's sigma space.
expectations: Uses comfy.samplers.KSampler or comfy.sample.sample_custom
Does not use the standard KSampler node — implements its own sampling loop
Converts user-provided float list to a sigma tensor correctly
Preserves LATENT dict keys (noise_mask, batch_index)
Clones the model before modifying model_options
Handles CFG scale as an input parameter
Uses comfy.sample.prepare_noise for noise generation
Returns a dict with 'samples' key (LATENT contract)

evals.prompt: I have a HuggingFace Diffusion Transformer (DiT) model from 'PixArt-Alpha/PixArt-XL-2-1024-MS' that I want to use as the UNet in a standard ComfyUI KSampler workflow. Write a custom node that loads this model from HuggingFace, wraps it so it's compatible with ComfyUI's ModelPatcher and sampling pipeline, and outputs a MODEL that can connect to KSampler. The model uses T5 text encoding and has a different forward signature than standard UNets. Handle the input/output tensor conversion, timestep handling, and device management properly.
evals.expected_output: A ComfyUI custom node package with a model loader node that downloads/loads the PixArt-Alpha DiT from HuggingFace, wraps it in an adapter class that matches ComfyUI's expected forward signature, wraps it in a ModelPatcher with proper load_device/offload_device, and registers it with ComfyUI's supported_models pattern if applicable. The output MODEL can connect directly to KSampler.
expectations: Loads the model using HuggingFace APIs (or describes the loading approach)
Creates an adapter wrapper that translates ComfyUI forward(x, timestep, context) to the model's expected signature
Wraps in ModelPatcher with correct device handling via model_management
Handles dtype conversion between the HF model and ComfyUI's tensor types
Sets up model.model_options with transformer_options
Returns MODEL type (ModelPatcher instance, not raw module)
Addresses how conditioning (text embeddings) will flow through the model
Does not hardcode the checkpoint path — uses user-provided path or hub download
